name: Goblins' Trail
name_original: Sierra Portillo de Junca — circular desde Herrera de Valdivielso
description: Hike the moderate 4.4-mile Sierra Portillo de Junca circular trail from Herrera de Valdivielso, featuring an enchanted forest and panoramic
region_intro: Hiking around Rucandio offers access to the diverse natural landscapes of Cantabria, Spain. The region is characterized by its proximity to the Picos de Europa National Park, featuring mountainous terrain, deep valleys, and extensive forests. Hikers can explore verdant valleys, such as the Pasiegos Valleys, and areas rich in water features and unique geological formations. Best hiking trails around Rucandio The most popular hiking route is Goblins' Trail, a 4.4 miles (7.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 23 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores local natural features. Another top favourite among local hikers is Peña Escalera Trail, a moderate 5.3 miles (8.5 km) path. This route offers varied terrain and takes approximately 2 hours 59 minutes. Local hikers also love the RutaDelArte4-Portico – ArtRoute3-Clover loop from Condado, a 7.3 miles (11.8 km) trail leading through local landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours 13 minutes. Hiking around Rucandio is defined by its mountainous areas, lush valleys, and extensive forests. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate walks to more challenging ascents. The routes in Rucandio are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 400 hikers have used komoot to explore Rucandio's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Rucandio?
A: There are over 60 hiking trails around Rucandio, offering a wide range of options from easy walks to more challenging ascents. The region's diverse landscapes, including mountains, valleys, and forests, provide varied terrain for all levels of hikers.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes near Rucandio?
A: Hiking around Rucandio features a mix of mountainous areas, lush green valleys, and extensive forests. You'll find trails traversing the varied landscapes of Cantabria, from the dramatic peaks of the Picos de Europa to verdant Pasiegos Valleys and ancient woodlands.

Q: Are there any easy, family-friendly hiking options in Rucandio?
A: Yes, Rucandio offers several easy routes suitable for families. For instance, the region has 12 easy trails, perfect for a leisurely outing. While specific family-friendly routes are not detailed here, the general availability of easy trails ensures options for all ages.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes around Rucandio?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes available. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15000666" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;RutaDelArte4-Portico – ArtRoute3-Clover loop from Condado&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 11.8 km trail that takes about 3 hours 13 minutes to complete, leading through local landscapes.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while hiking in the Rucandio area?
A: The Rucandio area is rich in natural features and landmarks. You can explore impressive peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5116707" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coronilla Summit (1,172 m)&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4431203" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Pilas&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5641331" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;AltoDeTesla&lt;/a&gt;. The region also boasts significant water features, including numerous rivers and waterfalls like the Churrón de Borleña Waterfall, and unique geological formations such as extensive cave systems.

Q: Are there any specific attractions or points of interest near Rucandio's hiking trails?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, you can discover historical sites and charming villages. Notable attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6365782" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Poza de la Sal Salt Flats and Old Railway Station&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6365773" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oña Historic Center&lt;/a&gt;, and the town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6318262" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trespaderne&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4988248" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santander–Mediterranean Greenway&lt;/a&gt; also offers a unique route for exploration.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Rucandio?
A: The trails in Rucandio are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 50 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, the stunning natural landscapes, and the well-maintained paths that lead through mountains, valleys, and forests.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Yes, Rucandio offers 9 difficult trails for experienced trekkers seeking a challenge. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15000684" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coronilla Summit (1,172 m) – Peña Escalera Viewpoint loop from Panizares&lt;/a&gt;, a demanding 18.3 km route with significant elevation gain, typically taking over 6 hours to complete.

Q: Can I access hiking trails in Rucandio using public transport?
A: While specific public transport links to individual trailheads are not detailed, Rucandio's location within Cantabria means that some areas are accessible by regional transport. It is advisable to check local bus schedules for routes connecting to villages or starting points of trails you plan to hike.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Rucandio?
A: Cantabria, where Rucandio is located, offers good hiking conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer is also popular, especially for higher altitude trails in areas like the Picos de Europa, while winter can offer unique, snow-covered landscapes for those prepared for colder conditions.

Q: Are there any routes that feature waterfalls?
A: Yes, the region is known for its water features. The Arroyo de la Llana River Trail, for example, showcases the beautiful Churrón de Borleña Waterfall. While specific routes are not all listed, the presence of numerous rivers and waterfalls throughout Cantabria suggests opportunities to encounter them on various hikes.

Q: Is there a local themed trail like 'The Route of the Elves' near Rucandio?
A: Yes, 'THE ROUTE OF THE ELVES' is noted as a local attraction just 2.9 km from downtown Rucandio. This suggests a themed hiking or walking path that could be a unique experience for visitors.

faqs.question: How long does it take to hike the Sierra Portillo de Junca — circular from Herrera de Valdivielso, and what is its difficulty?
faqs.answer: This circular hike typically takes around 2 hours and 23 minutes to complete. It is classified as a moderate trail, suitable for hikers with a good fitness level due to its varied terrain and elevation changes.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail, also known as the 'Ruta de los Duendes', starts and ends in Herrera de Valdivielso. You can usually find parking within the village, which serves as the primary access point for this route.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain and scenery can I expect on this hike?
faqs.answer: The trail offers diverse terrain, guiding you through pine forests, small oaks, and boxwood, as well as past cherry orchards. As you ascend, you'll be treated to stunning panoramic views of the Sierra de la Tesla and the Montes Obarenes. Sections include a rocky ridge and tracks, with the highlight being the 'Bosque Encantado'.

faqs.question: What unique features or points of interest will I encounter along the way?
faqs.answer: The most distinctive feature is the 'Bosque Encantado' (Enchanted Forest), where trees are adorned with colorful crocheted blankets as part of an 'Urban Knitting' art project. You'll also pass by the Twisted Tree of the Ruyales and various Kilometer Signs. The Enchanted Forest with Urban Knitting itself is a highlight.

faqs.question: Is the Sierra Portillo de Junca — circular from Herrera de Valdivielso trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas where wildlife like roe deer might be present, or when passing through agricultural areas.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: While the trail can be enjoyed in any season, spring is particularly recommended for seeing the cherry blossoms in bloom. The 'Bosque Encantado' offers a unique experience year-round.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail or the surrounding Valle de las Caderechas area?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Sierra Portillo de Junca — circular from Herrera de Valdivielso trail or to access the general Valle de las Caderechas area where it is located. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty and varied terrain, the trail is suitable for families with children who are accustomed to hiking and have a good fitness level. The 'Enchanted Forest' can be a particularly engaging feature for younger hikers.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is entirely part of the 'Ruta de los Duendes', which is a well-known local trail. You will be following this named path for the duration of your hike.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: We recommend bringing sturdy hiking shoes, sufficient water, snacks, sun protection, and a camera to capture the unique scenery of the 'Enchanted Forest' and panoramic views. Given the varied terrain, trekking poles might also be beneficial.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restaurants in Herrera de Valdivielso?
faqs.answer: Herrera de Valdivielso is a small village, but you may find local establishments offering refreshments or traditional meals. It's advisable to check opening hours in advance, especially outside of peak tourist seasons.
